text:
Model: B

Modeling pipeline

Prof. Ruiqi Zhang

1. Reference Collection & Model Blueprint

Objective: Gather accurate visual and dimensional references for modeling.

Steps:

Collect images of the object from multiple angles (front, back, top, side, perspective), then create blueprints for modeling reference.

If available, use 3D scanning to sample and extract accurate proportions from the original object.

Define the level of detail (LOD) required for the project (high-poly for baking, low-poly for Unity). A key feature for optimizing the rendering process in Unity.

2. 3D Modeling

Objective: Create a clean, optimized model based on reference images or scanned data.

Steps:

Use Blender, or other 3d software to model the object.

Maintain proper topology with clean quad-based geometry (avoid n-gons and unnecessary edge loops).

Ensure correct scale and proportions (all models should keep at scale 1:1:1), referencing the real-world dimensions or scanned data.

Separate parts logically (e.g., animated objects should be separate meshes).

Keep polygon count optimized for performance in Unity:

High-poly models (for baking normal maps) should have details.

Model: B

Low-poly models (for in-game use) should be retopologized if needed.

3. UV Unwrapping & Mapping

Objective: Prepare the model for texturing by creating an optimized UV layout.

Steps:

Unwrap the model in Blender, ensuring minimal stretching and distortion.

Maintain consistent texture density across all UV islands (average texture scale).

Pack UVs efficiently to maximize texture space usage. Bake all materials to one texture map. Need double check, the workflow for metal material.

4. Baking High-to-Low Poly Details (if applicable)

Objective: Transfer high-resolution details from a sculpted model to a game-ready low-poly version.

Steps:

Bake necessary texture maps:

Normal map (captures fine surface details).

Ambient occlusion (AO) map (enhances shading realism).

Curvature map (helps with edge wear in texturing).

Export baked maps in 4K or 2K resolution, depending on project needs.

Double-check the LOD workflow.

5. Texturing & Material Setup

Objective: Add PBR-based textures for realistic rendering in Unity.

Steps:

Use Substance Painter, Photoshop, or Blender for texturing.

If possible, use photographs as texture painting brushes for realistic details.

Model: B

Follow PBR workflow and create the following maps:

Base Color / Albedo

Roughness / Glossiness

Metallic / Specular

Normal Map

Ambient Occlusion (AO) (if applicable)

Test materials in a PBR shader inside Unity to ensure consistency.

6. Exporting & Optimizing for Unity

Objective: Prepare assets for smooth integration into Unity.

Steps:

Export low-poly models in FBX format.

Apply Transformations (Freeze Scale, Reset XForm, Apply Rotation & Scale) before export.

Export textures in PNG and ensure the format for Unity’s Standard Shader.

Test the model inside Unity, checking:

Scaling and orientation.

Material application.

Lighting behavior and shadow performance.

7. Documentation & Version Control

Objective: Keep track of asset progress and ensure smooth team collaboration.

Steps:

Store all assets, references, and project files in a shared drive (Google Drive, OneDrive).

Clearly label files with a naming convention:

ObjectName_ProjectName_ModelerName (e.g., RobotArm_VRroom_Ricky.fbx)
